changes_made: Changed k8s_apiserver_tls_enabled to k8s_apiserver_tls_encryption_enabled for clarity
Changed k8s_ingress_tls_enabled to k8s_ingress_tls_encryption_enabled for consistency
review_notes: The functions accurately map to cryptographic protection controls in Kubernetes. The naming conventions are mostly followed, but slight adjustments were made to ensure clarity and consistency with the pattern of indicating encryption explicitly.
naming_improvements: Ensure 'encryption' is explicitly mentioned in function names where applicable for clarity
title: Cryptographic Protection
step1_reasoning: The control SC-13 focuses on cryptographic protection, which in Kubernetes can be mapped to ensuring encryption at rest for secrets and etcd, as well as ensuring TLS is enabled for secure communication between components and ingress traffic. These checks ensure that sensitive data is protected both at rest and in transit within the Kubernetes environment.
